title_en: Introduction: Islamic Fiqh and Its Sources
content_en:
Al-Fiqh al-Islami wa-Adillatuhu by Dr. Wahbah al-Zuhayli stands as the most comprehensive contemporary encyclopedia of Islamic jurisprudence in the Arabic language. Written over many years and published in its expanded edition in eight volumes, the work covers virtually every chapter of classical fiqh across all four recognized Sunni schools of law, presenting each ruling alongside its evidence from the Quran, the Sunnah, scholarly consensus, and analogical reasoning. No comparable modern work matches its breadth, depth, and systematic organization.

The introductory section establishes the intellectual framework of the entire encyclopedia. Dr. al-Zuhayli defines fiqh in both its original broad sense — comprehensive understanding of the religion — and its technical jurisprudential sense: knowledge of the practical rulings of the Shari'ah derived from their detailed proofs. He traces the historical development of fiqh from the era of the Prophet, peace be upon him, through the Companions and Followers, the formation of the schools, the era of imitation (taqlid), and the contemporary period of revival and reform.

The sources of Islamic law are treated with scholarly rigor. The Quran as the primary source, the Sunnah as the second source that explains and complements the Quran, ijma' (scholarly consensus) and its various categories, and qiyas (analogical reasoning) with its conditions and types are all examined. Dr. al-Zuhayli also addresses secondary sources recognized by some or all schools: istihsan (juristic preference), maslaha mursalah (public interest), urf (custom), sadd al-dhara'i (blocking the means to harm), and others, explaining each school's acceptance or rejection of these secondary sources.

The introduction concludes with a discussion of the methodology of comparative fiqh — the analytical approach that Dr. al-Zuhayli employs throughout the encyclopedia. Rather than advocating blind following of one school, he presents the evidence for each school's position on contested questions, allowing readers to appreciate the richness of jurisprudential diversity while understanding that differences among the schools represent legitimate scholarly ijtihad rather than contradiction or error.
content_id:
Al-Fiqh al-Islami wa-Adillatuhu karya Dr. Wahbah al-Zuhayli merupakan ensiklopedia yurisprudensi Islam kontemporer paling komprehensif dalam bahasa Arab. Ditulis selama bertahun-tahun dan diterbitkan dalam edisi yang diperluas dalam delapan jilid, karya ini mencakup hampir setiap bab fiqih klasik dari keempat mazhab Sunni yang diakui, menyajikan setiap hukum beserta dalilnya dari Al-Quran, Sunnah, ijmak ulama, dan qiyas. Tidak ada karya modern yang menyamainya dalam keluasan, kedalaman, dan sistematika organisasinya.

Bagian pendahuluan meletakkan kerangka intelektual seluruh ensiklopedia. Dr. al-Zuhayli mendefinisikan fiqih dalam pengertian luas aslinya — pemahaman komprehensif terhadap agama — maupun pengertian teknis yurisprudensinya: pengetahuan tentang hukum-hukum praktis syariat yang diturunkan dari dalil-dalilnya yang terperinci. Beliau menelusuri perkembangan historis fiqih sejak era Nabi ﷺ melalui masa para Sahabat dan Tabi'in, pembentukan mazhab-mazhab, era taklid, hingga periode kebangkitan dan pembaruan kontemporer.

Sumber-sumber hukum Islam dibahas dengan kecermatan ilmiah. Al-Quran sebagai sumber utama, Sunnah sebagai sumber kedua yang menjelaskan dan melengkapi Al-Quran, ijmak (kesepakatan ulama) dan berbagai kategorinya, serta qiyas (penalaran analogis) beserta syarat dan jenisnya, semuanya ditelaah. Dr. al-Zuhayli juga membahas sumber-sumber sekunder yang diakui oleh sebagian atau semua mazhab: istihsan (preferensi yurisprudensial), maslahat mursalah (kepentingan publik), urf (adat), sadd adz-dzara'i (menutup jalan menuju kemudaratan), dan lainnya, dengan menjelaskan penerimaan atau penolakan masing-masing mazhab terhadap sumber-sumber sekunder tersebut.

Pendahuluan ditutup dengan pembahasan metodologi fiqih komparatif — pendekatan analitis yang diterapkan Dr. al-Zuhayli sepanjang ensiklopedia. Alih-alih menganjurkan taklid buta pada satu mazhab, beliau memaparkan dalil untuk setiap posisi mazhab pada persoalan-persoalan yang diperselisihkan, memungkinkan pembaca menghargai kekayaan keragaman yurisprudensial sambil memahami bahwa perbedaan antar mazhab merefleksikan ijtihad ulama yang sah, bukan kontradiksi atau kekeliruan.
content_ar: Al-الفقه al-Islami wa-Adillatuhu by Dr. Wahbah al-Zuhayli stands as the most comprehensive contemporary encyclopedia of Islamic jurisprudence in the Arabic language. Written over many years and published in its expanded edition in eight volumes, the work covers virtually every الباب of الكلاسيكي الفقه across all four recognized Sunni schools of law, presenting each ruling alongside its evidence from the القرآن الكريم, the السنة, العلمي consensus, and analogical reasoning. No comparable modern work matches its breadth, depth, and systematic organization. The introductory الفصل establishes the intellectual framework of the entire encyclopedia. Dr. al-Zuhayli defines الفقه in both its original broad sense — comprehensive الفهم of the religion — and its technical jurisprudential sense: العلم of the practical rulings of the Shari'ah derived from their detailed proofs. He traces the historical development of الفقه from the era of النبي ﷺ, ﷺ, through the الصحابة and Followers, the formation of the schools, the era of imitation (taqlid), and the contemporary period of revival and reform. The sources of Islamic law are treated with العلمي rigor. The القرآن الكريم as the الأساسي source, the السنة as the ثانياً source that explains and complements the القرآن الكريم, الإجماع' (العلمي consensus) and its various categories, and القياس (analogical reasoning) with its conditions and types are all examined. Dr. al-Zuhayli أيضاً addresses secondary sources recognized by some or all schools: istihsan (juristic preference), maslaha mursalah (public interest), urf (custom), sadd al-dhara'i (blocking the means to harm), and others, explaining each school's acceptance or rejection of these secondary sources. The introduction concludes with a discussion of the methodology of comparative الفقه — the analytical approach that Dr. al-Zuhayli employs throughout the encyclopedia. Rather than advocating blind following of one school, he presents the evidence for each school's position on contested questions, allowing readers to appreciate the richness of jurisprudential diversity بينما الفهم that differences among the schools represent legitimate العلمي الاجتهاد rather than contradiction or error.
